title: Publish v0.5.35
outOfScope: Publishing the tag or GitHub Release before the release-preparation PR is merged and remote checks pass.
Changing application/runtime behavior or governance policy.
Deleting release or Work Item evidence.

sources.reason: Requires release only after WI01-WI13 complete lifecycle closure.
Published release contract that must declare the promoted tag.
Canonical release lifecycle and source-commit evidence.
Authoritative release workflow for exact source, tag, assets, and provider evidence.
Release sequence, source identity, asset, SBOM, provenance, and digest requirements.
Previous release note used as the release-note format reference.

riskAssessment.reason: Release publication creates an immutable tag and provider assets; the workflow must bind every artifact to the verified default-branch source commit and fail closed on mismatch.

executionDecision.reason: User authorization explicitly includes release preparation, publication, verification, PR/merge, closure, and branch cleanup; provider publication remains gated by the merged PR and workflow evidence.

preReviewWarnings: Verify all prior Work Items are closed; promote only the exact next patch tag; bind source commit, remote/default branch, tag, release assets, SBOM, provenance, digest, workflow run, and strict smoke evidence; retain enterprise-security NO-GO wording.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: All WI01-WI13 archive, PR merge, ai-close-work-item, branch cleanup, and default-branch synchronization evidence is present before publication.
v0.5.35 is the exact next patch after published v0.5.34 and release metadata is internally consistent.
The release workflow publishes only the verified default-branch source commit and creates matching tag and provider assets.
SBOM, provenance, release-digests, source identity, workflow, and strict smoke verification are recorded or externally verified.
The release note and installation references do not claim provider evidence or enterprise assurance before it exists.
guidelines: Release metadata must remain machine-verifiable and source-bound.
Release evidence must distinguish local candidate baselines from provider-published assets.
Do not claim enterprise security, compliance, trusted identity, or production readiness from this release workflow.

intent.problem: WI01-WI13 are fully closed, and the prepared v0.5.35 candidate must now be promoted and published without mixing source commits, mutable branches, provider assets, or enterprise-assurance claims.
constraints: Use only the merged default-branch commit after WI13 closure.
Do not rewrite v0.5.34 or any historical tag.
Run local release-state, distribution, supply-chain, smoke, and quality checks before publication.
Provider publication is allowed only through the repository release workflow after the PR is merged.
intent.rationale: The release workflow is the controlled boundary that can create immutable tags and source-bound provider assets; repository metadata must be prepared and reviewed before invoking it.

rawUserRequest: 先走完当前文档的流程。用户已授权按计划逐工单执行，包含修改代码、测试、文档、PR、合并、ai-close-work-item、分支清理；妄想测试失败继续修改。

declaredIntent.summary: Prepare the next exact patch release v0.5.35 after WI01-WI13 closure, with source-bound provider evidence generated by the merged release workflow.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: all prior Work Items are archived, merged, closed, branch-cleaned, and default-branch synchronized
candidate and published release metadata are exact next-patch and internally consistent
provider tag/assets and source-bound evidence match the verified default-branch commit
scenarioCoverage.reason: Provider evidence is generated only by the merged release workflow after this preparation Work Item is reviewed.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly authorized release preparation/publication, verification, PR/merge, lifecycle closure, and branch cleanup.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
